The United States withdraws its last combat troops from Afghanistan.
The United States passes the USA PATRIOT Act in response to the 9/11 attacks.
The Human Genome Project announces the completion of the human genome map.
The European Union is established by the Maastricht Treaty.
Amazon is founded by Jeff Bezos and initially operates as an online bookstore.
The United Arab Emirates is unified by the British.
The world observes the first Earth Day, a global environmental movement.
The Soviet Union launches Salyut 7, the largest space station until the 1990s.
Facebook is founded by Mark Zuckerberg, Eduardo Saverin, and others.
Mandela becomes the first black president of South Africa, ending apartheid.
Nelson Mandela is released from prison after 27 years, a South African anti-apartheid icon.
Google acquires Android and begins development of the Android operating system.
Al Gore wins the Nobel Peace Prize for raising the alarm on climate change.
YouTube is founded by Chad Hurley, Steve Chen, and Jawed Karim.
John F. Kennedy is inaugurated as the 35th U.S. president
The first American in space is astronaut Alan Shepard
The Berlin Wall is erected, separating East and West Berlin
The Green Lantern comic book series is first published
The first Wal-Mart store opens in Rogers, Arkansas
The Soviet Union launches the first artificial satellite, Sputnik
The civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize
The Beatles release their debut album, Please Please Me
The first U.S. spacecraft, Mariner 4, flies by Mars
The first Ford Mustang is introduced at the World's Fair in New York
The 1967 Detroit riot, one of the deadliest in U.S. history, erupts
The U.S. Supreme Court rules on Loving v. Virginia, outlawing interracial marriage
The first heart transplant is performed by Dr. Christiaan Barnard
Soviet cosmonaut Alexei Leonov performs the first spacewalk
The first modern commercial airliner, the Boeing 727, enters service
The 1968 Democratic National Convention in Chicago turns violent
The first 747 jumbo jet is delivered to Pan Am Airlines
The first woman is ordained as a priest in the Episcopal Church of the U.S.
The Kent State shootings result in the deaths of four student protesters
The first Apple computer is produced by Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak
The first test-tube baby, Louise Brown, is born in the U.K.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft flies by Jupiter
The Sony Walkman is released, revolutionizing portable music
The first IBM PC is released, popularizing personal computing
The first U.S. space shuttle, Columbia, is launched
The first female bishop is consecrated in the Anglican Church
The world's first web browser, WorldWideWeb, is released
The first website is launched by the World Wide Web project
The first self-driving car is demonstrated by the U.S. Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA)
The first cell phone call is made from the first commercial cell phone network
The U.S. Senate votes to acquit President Bill Clinton on impeachment charges
The first Windows 2000 operating system is released by Microsoft
The dot-com bubble bursts, leading to a global economic downturn
The Mars Global Surveyor is launched by NASA to study the Martian surface
The first humanoid robot, Honda's ASIMO, is unveiled
The U.S. invades Afghanistan in response to the 9/11 terrorist attacks
The International Space Station is launched into orbit
The first YouTube video is uploaded by co-founder Jawed Karim
The Wikipedia online encyclopedia is launched by Jimmy Wales
The world's first 8-bit computer, the Commodore 64, is released
The first iPhone is released by Apple, revolutionizing smartphones
The Great Recession begins, causing a global economic downturn
The first high-definition Blu-ray discs are released for consumer playback
The first 3G wireless network is launched in Japan by NTT DoCoMo
The first Android smartphone is released by HTC
The first social media platform, Facebook, is opened to everyone aged 13 and above
The first self-driving car is demonstrated on public roads in California
The first iPad is released by Apple, popularizing tablets
The first iPhone 4S is released by Apple, introducing Siri virtual assistant
The Arab Spring protests begin in Tunisia and spread across the Middle East
The first Windows 8 operating system is released by Microsoft
The first Google Chromebook is released by Samsung
The first smartphone to use facial recognition, the Doro PhoneEasy 618, is released
The world's first 3D-printed house is built in the U.S.
The first smartphone with a fingerprint sensor, the iPhone 5S, is released
The world's first 1.5-megawatt solar farm is built in the U.S.
The world's first 3Gbps Wi-Fi router is released by Netgear
The first smartphone with a heart rate sensor, the Apple Watch, is released
The world's first quantum computer, the IBM Quantum Experience, is released
The first self-driving taxi service, Waymo One, is launched in Phoenix
The 2018 Winter Olympics in Pyeongchang, South Korea, feature AI-powered robots
The U.S. withdraws from the Paris Climate Agreement, signed in 2015
The world's first 5G network is launched in South Korea by SK Telecom
The first foldable smartphone, the Samsung Galaxy Fold, is released
The COVID-19 pandemic is declared a global health emergency by the World Health Organization
The first smartphone with a foldable display, the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip, is released
Nikita Khrushchev denounces Joseph Stalin at the 20th Soviet Communist Party Congress
Walt Disney's animated film The Jungle Book becomes a critically acclaimed success
The Soviet Union launches the unmanned lunar probe Luna 9, which sends back detailed images of the Moon
The United Kingdom passes the Immigration Act, which allows non-EU immigrants to settle in the country
Elton John releases the hit single Your Song, marking a commercial breakthrough for the artist
The United States launches the Pioneer 10 spacecraft, which is the first to visit the outer solar system
Richard Nixon signs the 26th Amendment to the US Constitution, lowering the voting age from 21 to 18
The United States Supreme Court upholds the death penalty in the case Gregg v. Georgia
The Soviet Union launches the Salyut 6 space station, which is occupied by cosmonauts for over a year
The United States and the Soviet Union sign the Helsinki Accords, which establish human rights and territorial integrity
Pac-Man, the iconic arcade game, is released by Namco in Japan
The United States and China sign the Sino-American Treaty, marking the beginning of diplomatic relations
IBM introduces the IBM PC, which becomes a dominant force in the personal computer market
The United States and Canada sign the Free Trade Agreement, creating a trade bloc between the two nations
The European Community adopts the Single European Act, which creates a single market for goods and services
The Soviet Union announces that it will withdraw its troops from Afghanistan, ending a nine-year occupation
The Voyager 1 spacecraft becomes the most distant human-made object in space, entering interstellar space
The Soviet Union holds its first free elections, marking a significant step towards democracy
The National AIDS Hotline is established in the United States, providing support to people with HIV/AIDS
The Hubble Space Telescope is launched into space, providing stunning images of the universe
The Maastricht Treaty creates the European Union, establishing a single currency and economic policy
The 7.0 Mw Northridge earthquake strikes the San Fernando Valley region of California
The United States and Russia sign the START II Treaty, reducing the number of nuclear warheads
The Netscape Navigator web browser is released, popularizing the World Wide Web
The United States and China sign the Chemical Weapons Convention, banning the use of chemical arms
The European Central Bank is established, marking a significant step towards European economic integration
The Mars Global Surveyor arrives at Mars, beginning a new era of robotic exploration
The International Committee of the Red Cross is awarded the Nobel Peace Prize
The United States launches a successful test of the Trident II missile
The International Space Station is launched, marking a significant step towards international cooperation in space
The European Union introduces the Euro, a single currency for member states
The United States and China sign the Shanghai Communiqué, marking a significant step towards Sino-American relations
The International Organization for Standardization releases the ISO 20000 standard for IT service management
The dot-com bubble bursts, leading to a significant decline in technology stocks
The International Space Station expands to include the Destiny laboratory
The United States and North Korea sign the Pledge of Amity and Cooperation
The International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor is announced, marking a significant step towards fusion energy
The United States and South Korea sign the Free Trade Agreement
The first human patient is treated with the first stem-cell derived therapy for blindness
The European Union introduces the European Constitution, which is later rejected by French and Dutch voters
The Mars Science Laboratory rover, Curiosity, is launched
The United States and Russia sign the New START Treaty, reducing the number of nuclear warheads
The European Union agrees to a package of bailouts for member states affected by the eurozone crisis
The NASA's Curiosity rover discovers evidence of ancient water on Mars
The European Central Bank launches a bond-buying program to combat the eurozone crisis
The International Space Station expands to include the Bigelow Expandable Activity Module
The United States and Cuba re-establish diplomatic relations after a half-century of isolation
The International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) begins construction in France
The European Union agrees to a package of reforms to address the eurozone crisis
The Chinese spacecraft Chang'e 3 lands on the Moon's surface
The European Union imposes sanctions on Russia in response to the annexation of Crimea
The United States and China sign the Paris Climate Agreement
The International Space Station conducts the first-ever deep spacewalk
The United States, Mexico, and Canada sign the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement
The European Space Agency's Rosalind Franklin rover is launched to Mars
The United States, Australia, and India sign a trilateral deal on cybersecurity
The European Union passes the General Data Protection Regulation, which regulates personal data online
The United States and South Korea sign the United States-South Korea Free Trade Agreement
The European Union launches the European Green Deal, a package of climate change policies
The International Space Station conducts the first-ever crewed spacewalk in spacewalks from the Russian segment
The European Union adopts the European Climate Law, which sets a goal to become carbon neutral by 2050
The International Space Station conducts the first-ever crewed spacewalk using the Russian Orlan spacesuit
The United States and the European Union sign the EU-US Privacy Shield agreement
The European Space Agency's JUICE mission is launched to explore Jupiter's icy moons
The European Union adopts the Digital Services Act, which regulates online platforms
The United States and the European Union sign the US-EU Trade and Technology Council
The European Space Agency's Gaia spacecraft releases its third data release, containing precise positions of over 1.8 billion stars
The International Space Station conducts the first-ever spacewalk from the International Docking Adapter
The European Union adopts the Media Literacy Strategy, which aims to promote media literacy across Europe
The United Nations adopts the Universal Declaration of Human Rights at the Paris Peace Conference
